article_title: AMD EPYC Captures Record 46.2 Percent of Server CPU Revenue as Data Center Business Surpasses Intel

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: AMD EPYC Captures Record 46.2 Percent of Server CPU Revenue as Data Center Business Surpasses Intel
content_preview.summary: Mercury Research data show AMD's EPYC processors claimed a record 46.2 percent of x86 server CPU revenue in Q1 2026, as AMD's data center segment hit $5.8 billion and overtook Intel's comparable unit.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

AMD's EPYC server processors reached a record 46.2 percent of x86 server CPU revenue in the first quarter of 2026, according to [Mercury Research data cited by Tom's Hardware](https://www.tomshardware.com/pc-components/cpus/amd-reaches-46-percent-of-server-x86-cpu-revenue-intel-still-controls-70-percent-of-the-consumer-pc-market-share). The result came alongside the first quarter in which AMD's data center segment revenue surpassed Intel's comparable unit — a milestone that marks th...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Briefing covering AMD's Q1 2026 server CPU market share milestone with appropriate factual depth. The article correctly distinguishes revenue share from unit share and provides useful context in the 'What We Don't Know' section. The Analysis section adds editorial value without crossing into advocacy.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched and read. source-0.html.gz (globenewswire.com - AMD Q1 2026 press release): confirmed $5.8B data center revenue up 57% YoY, $10.3B total revenue, Lisa Su quote verbatim, $11.2B Q2 guidance, 46% YoY growth, Meta 6 gigawatt Instinct GPU deployment, AWS/Google Cloud/Microsoft Azure/Tencent 5th Gen EPYC expansions. source-1.html.gz (tomshardware.com via Archive.org): confirmed 46.2% server CPU revenue share, Mercury Research attribution, 38.1% overall x86 CPU revenue share. source-2.html.gz (winbuzzer.com): confirmed AMD data center revenue crossover over Intel, 38.1% overall share, 46.2% server share. source-3.html.gz (tomshardware.com - Venice article): confirmed 256 cores, TSMC 2nm, 1.6 TB/s bandwidth (up from 614 GB/s), 70% more compute performance vs Turin, double CPU-to-GPU bandwidth. source-4.html.gz (tomshardware.com - enterprise roadmap): corroborating roadmap context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key figures verified: 46.2% server revenue share, 53.8% Intel share, $5.8B AMD data center revenue, $10.3B total revenue, 57% YoY growth, $11.2B Q2 guidance, 256-core Venice, 1.6 TB/s memory bandwidth, TSMC 2nm, 70% more compute. Lisa Su quote confirmed verbatim. No fabrications detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section offers historical context without editorializing. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate AMD/EPYC server revenue articles found in the archive. This covers new Q1 2026 data.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Publication-ready. All claims sourced and verified, Lisa Su quote exact, Venice specs confirmed. Clean submission.
